text: Refactoring means improving code structure without changing what it does. It is like cleaning a messy room. You do not add new furniture; you just organise things better. Developers refactor to make code more readable, which helps others understand it easily. They also fix "technical debt"—the messy shortcuts made when rushing to finish a project. Over time, technical debt slows down progress. By refactoring regularly, teams keep their code clean, efficient, and easy to maintain.
